title: Dysoxylum quercifolium
taxonomy: Dysoxylum quercifolium is classified within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. This family consists of approximately 290 species of trees and shrubs. Dysoxylum quercifolium is a species of plant native to the tropical forests of Asia. The genus Dysoxylum is a part of the larger clade of the Rutaceae order. The classification of Dysoxylum quercifolium is still evolving as new research and discoveries are made.
uses: Dysoxylum quercifolium has been studied for its potential medicinal uses, including the treatment of various health issues. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. These properties may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for human consumption. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential benefits of Dysoxylum quercifolium.
